text: Write a test line and see if it sticks: `paymentd-log hello; cat /var/log/paymentd/paymentd.log`. Empty. Now look closely at the file: `ls -l /var/log/paymentd/paymentd.log`.
The `ls -l` shows it's a symlink: `paymentd.log -> /dev/null`. Every write is discarded by design. Someone did this to silence a disk-space alert.

Replace the symlink with a real file: `sudo rm /var/log/paymentd/paymentd.log && sudo touch /var/log/paymentd/paymentd.log`. (Set sane ownership/mode if the app runs as a specific user.) Then `paymentd-log test && cat /var/log/paymentd/paymentd.log`.
